audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for divine jealousy, serpent deception, false apostles, unpaid ministry, angel of light, foolish boasting, hardship catalog, weakness boasting.

verses.restored: Would that you bear with me in a little foolishness; indeed, bear with me.
For I am jealous for you with Divine jealousy, for I betrothed you to one husband, to present you as a pure virgin to Messiah.
But I fear lest somehow, as the serpent deceived Eve in its craftiness, your thoughts might be corrupted from the simplicity and purity toward Messiah.

For if the one coming proclaims another Jesus whom we did not proclaim, or you receive a different spirit which you did not receive, or a different good news which you did not accept, you bear with it beautifully/well.
For I calculate myself to have been in no way inferior to the super-apostles.
But even if unskilled in word, yet not in knowledge; indeed in every way we made this manifest among all to you.
Or did I sin/misalign by humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I announced to you the good news of the Divine free of charge?
I stripped/received support from other assemblies, taking wages for service toward you.
And being present with you and lacking, I burdened no one; for the siblings coming from Macedonia filled up my lack, and in everything I kept myself unburdensome to you and will keep.
Truth of Messiah is in me, that this boasting will not be sealed off from me in the regions of Achaia.
Why? Because I do not love you? The Divine knows.
But what I do, I will also do, so that I may cut off opportunity from those desiring an opportunity, so that in what they boast they may be found just as we are.
For such ones are false apostles, deceitful workers, transforming themselves into apostles of Messiah.
And no wonder; for the adversarial one transforms itself into a messenger of light.
Therefore it is no great thing if its servants also transform themselves as servants of righteousness/justice, whose completion will be according to their works.
Again I say, let no one think me foolish; but if otherwise, receive me even as foolish, so that I also may boast a little.
What I speak, I do not speak according to the Lord, but as in foolishness, in this confidence of boasting.
Since many boast according to flesh, I also will boast.
For gladly you bear with the foolish, being wise.
For you bear it if someone enslaves you, if someone devours, if someone takes, if someone lifts oneself up, if someone strikes you in the face.
I speak according to dishonor, as though we have been weak. But in whatever anyone dares, in foolishness I speak, I also dare.
Are they Hebrews? I also. Are they Israelites? I also. Are they seed of Abraham? I also.
Are they servants of Messiah? I speak as if out of my mind: I more; in labors more abundantly, in prisons more abundantly, in blows beyond measure, in deaths often.
From Judeans five times I received forty lashes less one.
Three times I was beaten with rods; once I was stoned; three times I was shipwrecked; a night and day I have spent in the deep.
In journeys often, in dangers of rivers, dangers of robbers, dangers from my own people, dangers from nations, dangers in city, dangers in wilderness, dangers at sea, dangers among false siblings;
in labor and toil, in sleeplessness often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness.
Apart from the outer things, there is the daily pressure on me: the concern for all the assemblies.
Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to stumble, and I do not burn?
If it is necessary to boast, I will boast of the things of my weakness.
The Divine and Father of the Lord Jesus, who is blessed into the ages, knows that I am not lying.
In Damascus the ethnarch of Aretas the king was guarding the city of Damascenes to seize me,
and through a window I was lowered in a basket through the wall and escaped his hands.

translation_notes: Paul's foolish boasting is ironic and defensive, contrasting exploitative status-apostles with weakness-bearing service.
The adversarial one as messenger of light is a discernment warning against deceptive spiritual appearance, not a license to demonize people.
